creator: junovitch@freebsd.org
text:
I am seeing the correct behavior with the following lines in /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/make.conf.  In each case the `pkg info` shows the testport built the correct ports.

DEFAULT_VERSIONS= ruby=2.1 pgsql=9.4 perl5=5.22 php=5.4

# pkg info 
codeigniter-3.0.3              Framework for developing PHP web applications
libltdl-2.4.6                  System independent dlopen wrapper
libmcrypt-2.5.8_3              Multi-cipher cryptographic library (used in PHP)
libxml2-2.9.3                  XML parser library for GNOME
pcre-8.37_4                    Perl Compatible Regular Expressions library
php5-5.4.45                    PHP Scripting Language
php5-mcrypt-5.4.45             The mcrypt shared extension for php
php5-session-5.4.45            The session shared extension for php
pkg-1.6.2                      Package manager

DEFAULT_VERSIONS= ruby=2.1 pgsql=9.4 perl5=5.22 php=5.5

# pkg info 
codeigniter-3.0.3              Framework for developing PHP web applications
libltdl-2.4.6                  System independent dlopen wrapper
libmcrypt-2.5.8_3              Multi-cipher cryptographic library (used in PHP)
libxml2-2.9.3                  XML parser library for GNOME
pcre-8.37_4                    Perl Compatible Regular Expressions library
php55-5.5.30                   PHP Scripting Language
php55-mcrypt-5.5.30            The mcrypt shared extension for php
php55-session-5.5.30           The session shared extension for php
pkg-1.6.2                      Package manager


I notice you are building codeigniter-2.2.4 from 3 months ago.  Can you replicate this on a current release?  What revision is your ports tree?  What version of Poudriere is in use?
